title: Nowcasting a reporting triangle with csfmt_ensemble_v3
text: Real surveillance data arrives delay: case reference week W may reported week W, W+1, W+2, … simulate 70 weeks week’s cases within week two tail dribbles month. Wrap csfmt_reporting_triangle_v3. as_of boundary (latest week know ) inferred newest reporting week.
code: weeks   <- cstime::dates_by_isoyearweek$isoyearweek i0      <- match("2023-01", weeks) max_delay  <- 5L n_weeks    <- 70L delay_prob <- c(0.45, 0.30, 0.15, 0.07, 0.03)     # P(delay = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 weeks)  set.seed(1) rows <- lapply(seq_len(n_weeks), function(w) {   ref_i <- i0 + w - 1L   n     <- rpois(1, 60 + 25 * sin(2 * pi * w / 52))   # a seasonal signal   delay <- sample(0:(max_delay - 1L), n, replace = TRUE, prob = delay_prob)   data.table(isoyearweek_reference = weeks[ref_i], rep_i = ref_i + delay) }) ll <- rbindlist(rows) ll[, isoyearweek_reporting := weeks[rep_i]]  # "today" is the last reference week: drop everything not reported yet, so the # most recent weeks are still incomplete (right-truncated) -- exactly the problem # a nowcast solves. as_of_i <- i0 + n_weeks - 1L ll <- ll[rep_i <= as_of_i]  triangle_long <- ll[, .(numerator = .N),                     by = .(isoyearweek_reference, isoyearweek_reporting)] triangle_long[, `:=`(indicator = "example", location = "nation",                      age = "total", sex = "total")] head(triangle_long) #>    isoyearweek_reference isoyearweek_reporting numerator indicator location #>                   <char>                <char>     <int>    <char>   <char> #> 1:               2023-01               2023-04         4   example   nation #> 2:               2023-01               2023-01        23   example   nation #> 3:               2023-01               2023-03        10   example   nation #> 4:               2023-01               2023-02        20   example   nation #> 5:               2023-01               2023-05         1   example   nation #> 6:               2023-02               2023-03        17   example   nation #>       age    sex #>    <char> <char> #> 1:  total  total #> 2:  total  total #> 3:  total  total #> 4:  total  total #> 5:  total  total #> 6:  total  total tri <- csfmt_reporting_triangle_v3(   triangle_long,   id_cols       = c("indicator", "location", "age", "sex"),   reference_col = "isoyearweek_reference",   reporting_col = "isoyearweek_reporting",   value_col     = "numerator" ) attr(tri, "as_of") #> [1] "2024-18"

title: Nowcasting a reporting triangle with csfmt_ensemble_v3
text: nowcast_quasipoisson_v1() regresses settled week’s final total counts reported far, simulates incomplete recent weeks. returns csfmt_ensemble_v3: one row per reference week n_sim-column matrix draws (completed) weekly total. Settled weeks point masses observed total; recent weeks carry completion uncertainty. Collapse draws quantile summary. recent weeks widening interval (fewer reports ), older weeks pinned settled total:
code: ens <- nowcast_quasipoisson_v1(tri, max_delay = max_delay, n_sim = 500) ens #> <csfmt_ensemble_v3> 70 rows | 1 series | draws: numerator_nowcasted q <- ens_collapse(ens, probs = c(0.05, 0.5, 0.95)) tail(q[, .(isoyearweek,            lo  = numerator_nowcasted_q05x0,            med = numerator_nowcasted_q50x0,            hi  = numerator_nowcasted_q95x0)], 8) #>    isoyearweek    lo   med     hi #>         <char> <num> <num>  <num> #> 1:     2024-11 70.00    70  70.00 #> 2:     2024-12 86.00    86  86.00 #> 3:     2024-13 88.00    88  88.00 #> 4:     2024-14 77.00    77  77.00 #> 5:     2024-15 77.00    79  95.00 #> 6:     2024-16 58.00    65  78.00 #> 7:     2024-17 70.00    85 102.00 #> 8:     2024-18 53.95    67  84.05 show <- tail(q, 30) xs   <- seq_len(nrow(show)) plot(xs, show$numerator_nowcasted_q50x0, type = "n",      ylim = range(show$numerator_nowcasted_q05x0, show$numerator_nowcasted_q95x0),      xlab = "week (last 30)", ylab = "weekly total (nowcast)") polygon(c(xs, rev(xs)),         c(show$numerator_nowcasted_q05x0, rev(show$numerator_nowcasted_q95x0)),         col = grDevices::adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.25), border = NA) lines(xs, show$numerator_nowcasted_q50x0, lwd = 2, col = "steelblue4")

what: Is the nowcast any good? A replay backtest
title: Nowcasting a reporting triangle with csfmt_ensemble_v3
text: reporting triangle records every count arrived, can reconstruct exactly known past week replay method . “method” function f(triangle) -> ensemble parameters baked ; nowcast_evaluate_v1() replays scores result two scale-free diagnostics per horizon (weeks-back -week; 0 = current, least-observed week): coverage — 90% / 50% interval actually contain settled truth often? (interval honesty; target 0.90 / 0.50) revision — far published median sits eventually settles, fraction truth: signed (bias), absolute (typical move), 5–95% band, tail probabilities. Pass named list methods instead one, replayed paired draws (shared seed) stacked method column, can race several engines head--head.
code: method <- function(x) nowcast_quasipoisson_v1(x, max_delay = max_delay, n_sim = 500) as_of_weeks <- utils::tail(weeks[i0:as_of_i], 30)     # replay the last 30 weeks nowcast_evaluate_v1(tri, method, max_delay = max_delay,                     as_of_weeks = as_of_weeks, horizons = 0:3, seed = 1) #>    horizon     n coverage_50 coverage_90 median_signed median_abs     q05 #>      <int> <int>       <num>       <num>         <num>      <num>   <num> #> 1:       3    29       1.000       1.000        0.0000     0.0213 -0.0503 #> 2:       2    28       0.857       1.000       -0.0100     0.0375 -0.1151 #> 3:       1    27       0.741       0.926        0.0132     0.0536 -0.1733 #> 4:       0    26       0.462       0.808       -0.0844     0.1328 -0.2617 #>       q95 p_gt_25 p_gt_50 method #>     <num>   <num>   <num> <char> #> 1: 0.0305  0.0000       0 method #> 2: 0.0677  0.0000       0 method #> 3: 0.1261  0.0370       0 method #> 4: 0.3085  0.1923       0 method

text: Every measure column self-describing name built parsed grammar, downstream code never hard-codes strings.
code: csfmt_var("numerator", role = "nowcasted", q = 0.5)   # -> the collapsed median column #> [1] "numerator_nowcasted_q50x0" csfmt_parse("numerator_nowcasted_q50x0")              # -> back to its components #> $measure #> [1] "numerator" #>  #> $role #> [1] "nowcasted" #>  #> $q #> [1] 0.5

title: Nowcasting a reporting triangle with csfmt_ensemble_v3
text: Add rate ens_add_rate() (numerator vs nowcasted denominator), short-term trend short_term_trend(), MEM intensity thresholds mem_thresholds_v1(), exceedance detection signal_detection_hlm() — just adds draw columns collapse. ens_collapse(heal = TRUE) returns cstidy::csfmt_rts_data_v3 ready standard plotting/table helpers. Race candidate engines head--head passing several methods nowcast_evaluate_v1() comparing coverage / revision.

text: Trend status can mapped bar fill colours, error bars showing prediction interval.  Point shapes can used instead, placing symbol bar mark trend category.
code: q <- ggplot(   res,   aes(     x = isoyearweek,     y = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_n,     group = 1   ) ) q <- q + geom_col(mapping = aes(fill = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_trend0_41_status)) q <- q + geom_errorbar(   mapping = aes(     ymin = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_predinterval_q02x5_n,     ymax = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_predinterval_q97x5_n   ) ) q <- q + scale_y_continuous("Weekly hospitalization with Covid-19 as primary cause", expand = c(0, 0.1)) q <- q + scale_x_discrete("Isoyearweek") q <- q + expand_limits(y=0) q <- q + scale_fill_brewer("6 week trend", palette = "Set1") q shape_adjustment_factor <- max(res$hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_n)*0.01 q <- ggplot(   res,   aes(     x = isoyearweek,     y = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_n,     group = 1   ) ) q <- q + geom_col() q <- q + geom_point(mapping = aes(   y = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_n + shape_adjustment_factor,   shape = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_trend0_41_status )) q <- q + geom_errorbar(   mapping = aes(     ymin = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_predinterval_q02x5_n,     ymax = hospitalization_with_covid19_as_primary_cause_forecasted_predinterval_q97x5_n   ) ) q <- q + scale_y_continuous("Weekly hospitalization with Covid-19 as primary cause", expand = c(0, 0.1)) q <- q + scale_x_discrete("Isoyearweek") q <- q + expand_limits(y=0) q <- q + scale_shape_manual("6 week trend", values = c("Increasing" = 17, "Decreasing" = 6)) q #> Warning: Removed 91 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: Version 2026.7.1
text: reporting_completion_trend_v1 returns completion curve calendar year () + month (last N, per series) scope column – year/month trend luftveis pipeline used assemble hand. nowcast_evaluate_v1 now single entry point scoring nowcasts: give triangle one method (NAMED list), replays – paired shared seed – returns one per-horizon table interval coverage + point-estimate revision, method column. subsumes former nowcast_score (coverage, via scoringutils), nowcast_revision, nowcast_compare nowcast_validate; coverage read straight interval quantiles, scoringutils longer dependency (WIS dropped). Removed unused nowcast_survrtrunc_v1 engine (flexsurv dependency) conformal calibration functions (nowcast_estimate_calibration_v1 / _apply_ / print.nowcast_calibration) — dropped production pipeline. Renamed mem_thresholds -> mem_thresholds_v1 (versioned-engine convention).

title: Version 2026.7.1
text: New vignette “Nowcasting reporting triangle csfmt_ensemble_v3” — runnable end--end walk-(synthetic triangle -> nowcast -> collapse -> backtest coverage/revision/completion -> naming grammar). Documented previously-bare print.csfmt_ensemble_v3 method; added missing @returns ensemble/nowcast S3 methods (mem_thresholds_v1, short_term_trend, signal_detection_hlm, nowcast_quasipoisson_v1). Added runnable @examples naming-grammar functions (csfmt_var, csfmt_parse, q_label, q_value) nowcast analysis functions (nowcast_evaluate_v1, reporting_completion_trend_v1). Fixed unescaped % nowcast / reporting_completion roxygen corrupting generated .Rd, plus copy-paste errors simulation-helper docs.

title: Version 2026.7.1
text: new draw-parallel ensemble format full analysis pipeline built (reporting triangle -> nowcast -> trend -> MEM thresholds -> quantile collapse). csfmt_ensemble_v3: S3 container ($data data.table + per-measure $draws matrices) invariants enforced validate_ensemble. Naming grammar: csfmt_var / csfmt_parse (order-independent parse trailing coordinates), q_label / q_value, csfmt_interpret self-describing datasets. csfmt_reporting_triangle_v3: reference--delay input format reshape completes reference axis. nowcast(): reporting triangle -> ensemble via flexsurv survrtrunc + negbin (epinowcast dependency). Can nowcast denominator alongside numerator (full % positive -> MEM path) surface observed denominator total. reporting--reference check NA-safe. observed_ensemble(): passthrough (degenerate single-draw) ensemble indicators nowcasted. collapse(): ensemble -> quantile summary; collapse(heal = TRUE) heals result cstidy csfmt_rts_data_v3. add_rate(): numerator-vs-denominator rate (% positive). short_term_trend.csfmt_ensemble: batched, shared-design-matrix kernel also emits P(increasing). mem_thresholds.csfmt_ensemble_v3: MEM intensity thresholds provisional seasons; exclude_seasons drops anomalous seasons baseline; training capped recent .seasons na.omit; non-zero-season guard plus quiet memmodel wrapper prevent sparse indicators erroring. signal_detection_hlm.csfmt_ensemble_v3: per-draw exceedance detection. add_holiday_effect ensemble format. Input QC: qc_surveillance_data (generic input QC, verdict ) qc_week_over_week (/B revision comparison across runs). add_rate collapse renamed ens_add_rate / ens_collapse now S3 generics dispatching csfmt_ensemble_v3 (ens_ family), ensemble class carries “operates ensemble” meaning. Behaviour unchanged. (add_holiday_effect simulation-data helper plain data.table keeps name.) nowcast observed_ensemble renamed nowcast_survrtrunc_v1 nowcast_passthrough_to_ensemble_v1 – concrete, VERSIONED nowcast engines share contract f(reporting_triangle, ...) -> csfmt_ensemble_v3. Behaviour unchanged; _vN suffix versions algorithm (future nowcast_stan_v1 nowcast_simple_v2 slots beside ), selected caller-side registry. validation harness (nowcast_backtest/score/compare/validate/censor/truth) generic tooling versioned. nowcast_survrtrunc_v1 gains delay_window (default 26 weeks): reporting-delay distribution estimated recent weeks, non-stationary / drifting delay (e.g. backfilled history live prospective reporting) tracked instead averaged stale pooled curve. Fixes median bias caused sub-nominal interval coverage; residual -coverage (plug-delay) documented calibration test awaits per-draw delay uncertainty backtest-driven recalibration. NULL restores old pool--history behaviour. Calibration test (test-nowcast-calibration.R): empirical interval coverage vs nominal synthetic data, stationary case (calibrated) drifting-delay case (reproduces real-data -coverage synthetically). nowcast_simple_v1 renamed nowcast_survrtrunc_v1 (name now states method: right-truncated survival delay + negbin completion). New engine nowcast_quasipoisson_v1: discriminative (regression) nowcast – horizon, regress settled TOTAL counts reported far (total ~ n[delay 0] + n[delay 1] + ..., quasipoisson/identity, intercept) recent settled weeks, simulate incomplete weeks (parameter uncertainty fit + dispersion-matched negbin). per-week magnitude parameter, robust recent weeks honestly dispersed – drifting-delay synthetic coverage ~0.79 vs plug-survrtrunc’s ~0.72 (nominal 0.90). Base stats ; f(triangle) -> ensemble contract -> drops registry candidate key. Backtest-driven recalibration: nowcast_estimate_calibration_v1 learns per-group (default horizon) conformal interval-scaling correction past nowcasts vs settled truth, nowcast_apply_calibration_v1 applies method’s intervals hit nominal coverage regardless internal misspecification (nowcast_calibration S3 object print method sits ). Turns backtest calibration data: engine -> backtest -> estimate -> apply -> honest intervals. Distribution-free (split conformal); estimate past backtests, apply current nowcast. Nowcast validation harness (method-agnostic, replay-based): nowcast_censor (reconstruct known -past week reporting triangle), nowcast_truth (settled totals), nowcast_backtest (replay f(triangle) -> ensemble across -weeks tidy quantile nowcasts), nowcast_evaluate_v1 (score one several methods interval coverage + point-estimate revision horizon – see Simplification section ). reporting_completion: empirical reporting-delay summary triangle – settled weeks, mean delay, weeks-observed reach 25/50/75/90/95% reference week’s cases, fraction actually max_delay. period = "year" / "month" stratifies settled weeks time (ISO year / midweek-day month) drift reporting speed shows trend mean delay instead averaged away.
